en.extract:
The eyebrowed heleia (Heleia superciliaris), also known as the cream-browed white-eye, cream-browed ibon or yellow-browed white-eye, is a species of bird in the white-eye family Zosteropidae.  It is endemic to the Lesser Sunda Islands. Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist montane forest.

The '''eyebrowed heleia''' ('''''Heleia superciliaris'''''), also known as the '''cream-browed white-eye''',<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/new/ioc-lists/master-list-2/ |title=Master Lists: v15.1 |website=[[IOC World Bird List]] |publisher=[[International Ornithologists' Union]] |access-date=13 March 2025}}</ref> '''cream-browed ibon''' or '''yellow-browed white-eye''', is a species of [[bird]] in the white-eye family [[Zosteropidae]].  It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to the [[Lesser Sunda Islands]]. Its natural [[habitat]] is subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]].
